title: Listening - Part 1 - Exercise 8
description: You are going to hear people talking in eight situations. For questions 1–8, choose the correct answer (A, B or C). Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear each piece twice. For questions 1–8, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a message about an English language examination.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Hi Yasmin, it’s Mrs Patel from the language centre. Your speaking exam is still on Friday at nine thirty, and you should arrive fifteen minutes early with your identification. ||Woman: The examiners are using another part of the building this week, so [1]go straight to Room 24 on the second floor instead of reporting to reception[/1]. There will be signs by the stairs.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Hi Yasmin, it’s Mrs Patel from the language centre. Your speaking exam is still on Friday at nine thirty, and you should arrive fifteen minutes early with your identification.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The examiners are using another part of the building this week, so go straight to Room 24 on the second floor instead of reporting to reception. There will be signs by the stairs.

questions.question: Where should Yasmin go for her speaking exam?
questions.explanation: Mrs Patel instructs Yasmin to go directly to Room 24 on the second floor (evidence [1]).

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a mountain walk.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: You looked worried before yesterday’s mountain walk. Was the route difficult? ||Girl: It wasn’t too hard, actually, and the views were wonderful when the clouds cleared. ||Boy: So why were you nervous at first? ||Girl: [2]The weather report had warned of heavy rain, and I was afraid we might be caught in a storm[/2]. Fortunately, our guide checked conditions carefully before we set off.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: You looked worried before yesterday’s mountain walk. Was the route difficult?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: It wasn’t too hard, actually, and the views were wonderful when the clouds cleared.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: So why were you nervous at first?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: The weather report had warned of heavy rain, and I was afraid we might be caught in a storm. Fortunately, our guide checked conditions carefully before we set off.

questions.question: Why was the girl worried before the walk?

options.A: She was unsure about the weather.
options.B: She did not trust the guide.
options.C: She expected the route to be too long.

questions.explanation: The girl was concerned because the forecast had warned of heavy rain and a possible storm (evidence [2]).

extracts.context: You hear an announcement in a shopping centre.
extracts.audio_script: Announcer: Shoppers leaving by car this afternoon should note that repairs are taking place near the usual western exit. Parking levels one and two are open as normal, and there is no charge for the first hour. ||Announcer: Until six o’clock, [3]all vehicles must leave through the eastern exit beside the supermarket, where staff will direct traffic[/3]. We apologise for any delay.

dialogue.speaker: announcer
dialogue.text: Shoppers leaving by car this afternoon should note that repairs are taking place near the usual western exit. Parking levels one and two are open as normal, and there is no charge for the first hour.

dialogue.speaker: announcer
dialogue.text: Until six o’clock, all vehicles must leave through the eastern exit beside the supermarket, where staff will direct traffic. We apologise for any delay.

questions.question: What must drivers do this afternoon?

options.A: pay for parking before they leave
options.B: use a different exit from the car park
options.C: move their cars to another level

questions.explanation: Drivers are instructed to leave through the eastern exit because repairs affect the usual one (evidence [3]).

extracts.context: You hear a radio interview with a teenage swimmer.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: You competed in your first early-morning swimming event last weekend, Alex. Was the race itself difficult? ||Boy: It was tiring, but I’d trained for the distance and knew I could finish. [4]The hardest thing was getting up at half past four and eating breakfast when I still felt asleep[/4]. Once I reached the pool, I was fine and really enjoyed the competition.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: You competed in your first early-morning swimming event last weekend, Alex. Was the race itself difficult?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: It was tiring, but I’d trained for the distance and knew I could finish. The hardest thing was getting up at half past four and eating breakfast when I still felt asleep. Once I reached the pool, I was fine and really enjoyed the competition.

questions.question: What did Alex find most difficult?

options.C: preparing for such an early start

questions.explanation: Alex says the hardest part was waking and eating breakfast very early in the morning (evidence [4]).

extracts.context: You hear a customer talking to an assistant in a luggage shop.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: I like this large suitcase, but I’m travelling with an airline that has strict size limits for cabin bags. ||Assistant: In that case, this smaller case is safer. It meets their current requirements and still has plenty of space inside. ||Woman: [5]I don’t want to pay an extra charge at the airport, so I’ll take the smaller one[/5]. ||Assistant: Fine. It also has a five-year guarantee.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I like this large suitcase, but I’m travelling with an airline that has strict size limits for cabin bags.

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: In that case, this smaller case is safer. It meets their current requirements and still has plenty of space inside.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I don’t want to pay an extra charge at the airport, so I’ll take the smaller one.

dialogue.speaker: assistant
dialogue.text: Fine. It also has a five-year guarantee.

questions.question: Why does the woman buy the smaller suitcase?

options.B: It is available in a better colour.
options.C: It should avoid an airline charge.

questions.explanation: She chooses the smaller case so that she will not have to pay an extra airline fee (evidence [5]).

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking before a meeting.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you printed copies of the agenda for this afternoon’s meeting? The manager asked us to bring twelve. ||Man: I was going to, but the printer has stopped working and the technician won’t arrive until tomorrow. ||Woman: That’s a problem. ||Man: [6]I’ll email the agenda to everyone now, so they can read it on their laptops during the meeting[/6]. ||Woman: Good solution.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you printed copies of the agenda for this afternoon’s meeting? The manager asked us to bring twelve.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I was going to, but the printer has stopped working and the technician won’t arrive until tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That’s a problem.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ll email the agenda to everyone now, so they can read it on their laptops during the meeting.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Good solution.

questions.question: What will the man do about the agenda?

options.A: ask the manager to cancel the meeting
options.C: print it in another office tomorrow

questions.explanation: Since the printer is broken, the man decides to email the agenda to everyone (evidence [6]).

extracts.context: You hear two housemates making plans for Saturday.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: Shall we go to the beach on Saturday? I haven’t been there all summer. ||Boy: I’d love to, but the forecast says it’s going to be cold and windy. ||Girl: Then perhaps we need an indoor plan. ||Boy: [7]There’s a new climbing centre near the station; we could book a beginners’ session there instead[/7]. ||Girl: Great. I’ll check the times online tonight.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Shall we go to the beach on Saturday? I haven’t been there all summer.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I’d love to, but the forecast says it’s going to be cold and windy.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Then perhaps we need an indoor plan.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: There’s a new climbing centre near the station; we could book a beginners’ session there instead.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Great. I’ll check the times online tonight.

questions.question: What do the speakers plan to do on Saturday?

options.B: spend the day at the beach

questions.explanation: They decide to book a beginners’ session at an indoor climbing centre instead of going to the beach (evidence [7]).

extracts.context: You hear a presenter talking about a local arts centre.
extracts.audio_script: Presenter: Brook Arts Centre is offering new weekend courses in painting, dance and digital photography from next month. Anyone can visit the free exhibition in the entrance hall, and the café is now open on Sundays too. ||Presenter: For the next two weeks, [8]students under eighteen who book a course online will receive a twenty-percent reduction in the course fee[/8]. Full details are available on the centre’s website.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: Brook Arts Centre is offering new weekend courses in painting, dance and digital photography from next month. Anyone can visit the free exhibition in the entrance hall, and the café is now open on Sundays too.

dialogue.speaker: presenter
dialogue.text: For the next two weeks, students under eighteen who book a course online will receive a twenty-percent reduction in the course fee. Full details are available on the centre’s website.

questions.question: What does the arts centre offer young students?

options.A: free entry to all weekend courses
options.B: cheaper online course bookings for a limited period
options.C: a paid job in its Sunday café

questions.explanation: Students under eighteen receive a twenty-percent discount if they book a course online during the stated period (evidence [8]).
